title: Dandy-Walker Malformation: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Dandy-Walker Malformation
Identify key diagnostic features of Dandy-Walker Malformation
Apply clinical reasoning to Dandy-Walker Malformation management
Recognize common pitfalls in Dandy-Walker Malformation

sections.title: Overview of Dandy-Walker Malformation
sections.content:
**Dandy-Walker Malformation**

Posterior fossa abnormalities, cerebellar vermis hypoplasia, hydrocephalus association

**Key Points:**
• Classic triad: cystic dilation of 4th ventricle + complete/partial absence of cerebellar vermis + enlarged posterior fossa
• Hydrocephalus present in 80% of cases
• Dandy-Walker variant: less severe, partial vermis hypoplasia, less posterior fossa enlargement

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Classic triad: cystic dilation of 4th ventricle + complete/partial absence of cerebellar vermis + enlarged posterior fossa
• Hydrocephalus present in 80% of cases

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Dandy-Walker variant: less severe, partial vermis hypoplasia, less posterior fossa enlargement
• Associated with chromosomal anomalies, other brain malformations
• Treatment: CSF diversion (VP shunt or cyst-peritoneal shunt)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Dandy-Walker Malformation:**

• Dandy-Walker variant: less severe, partial vermis hypoplasia, less posterior fossa enlargement
• Associated with chromosomal anomalies, other brain malformations
• Treatment: CSF diversion (VP shunt or cyst-peritoneal shunt)

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Dandy-Walker Malformation?

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases
Classic triad: cystic dilation of 4th ventricle + complete/partial absence of cerebellar vermis + enlarged posterior fossa
options.explanation: Classic triad: cystic dilation of 4th ventricle + complete/partial absence of cerebellar vermis + enlarged posterior fossa

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Classic triad: cystic dilation of 4th ventricle + complete/partial absence of cerebellar vermis + enlarged posterior fossa.

correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Classic triad: cystic dilation of 4th ventricle + complete/partial absence of cerebellar vermis + enlarged posterior fossa

coachDx.misconception: While typically self-resolving without treatment may seem plausible, it does not match the clinical scenario described.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Classic triad: cystic dilation of 4th ventricle + complete/partial absence of ce from Typically self-resolving without treatment.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Classic triad: cystic dilation of 4th ventricle + complete/partial absence of cerebellar vermis + en.

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Treatment: CSF diversion (VP shunt or cyst-peritoneal shunt).
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
